market_context: Markets are showing cautious optimism with <cite index="1-1,4-1">S&P 500 futures up 0.24% and Dow futures up 0.38%</cite> amid <cite index="11-8,15-2">renewed hopes for U.S.-Iran peace talks</cite>. <cite index="21-4,21-10,21-11">TSMC reported strong Q1 earnings with 58% profit growth but stock declined</cite>, while <cite index="32-7,36-16">Netflix beat Q1 estimates but fell 9-10% after hours on disappointing Q2 guidance</cite>.

predictions.reasoning: <cite index="1-1,4-1">S&P 500 futures are up 0.24% in premarket trading</cite> and <cite index="15-16,26-20">overall market optimism is driven by hopes of progress in US-Iran peace talks</cite>. The geopolitical risk premium may continue to compress if diplomatic progress continues, supporting a modest upward move in the broader market.

<cite index="21-4,22-10,26-10">Despite TSMC beating Q1 estimates with 58% profit growth, shares are down 1.9% in premarket</cite>. <cite index="21-5,21-26">This reflects how astronomical expectations have weighed on chipmaker stocks</cite>, suggesting the strong results were already priced in and profit-taking will continue.

<cite index="32-7,36-16">Netflix fell 9-10% in after-hours trading despite beating Q1 estimates</cite> because <cite index="32-7">Q2 guidance missed expectations</cite>. <cite index="37-13,37-14">Given elevated expectations, forward guidance drives post-earnings reactions and failure to raise guidance could trigger profit-taking</cite>.

<cite index="22-15,26-14">Chip stocks are leading losses on the Nasdaq with Nvidia, ASML and Micron all lower as investors take profits after the recent rally</cite>. <cite index="21-25,21-26">The failure of TSMC and ASML to rally on strong earnings could be a bellwether for the wider chip industry</cite>, suggesting sector rotation out of semiconductors despite strong fundamentals.

<cite index="4-1">Nasdaq futures are up 0.18%</cite> and <cite index="26-21,26-22">tech titans have been on "an absolute tear" since end of March with investors continuing to "buy the dip"</cite>. Despite some chip stock weakness, the broader tech sector momentum and geopolitical optimism should support modest gains in the tech-heavy QQQ.
